records.ety: [AS. mīte mite (in sense 1); akin to LG. mite, D. mijt, G. miete, OHG. mīza; cf. Goth. maitan to cut.]

defs: A minute arachnid, of the order Acarina, of which there are many species; as, the dust mite, cheese mite, sugar mite, harvest mite, three-toed spider mite, etc. See Acarina.
A small coin formerly circulated in England, rated at about a third of a farthing. The name is also applied to a small coin used in Palestine in the time of Christ.
quotes: Two mites, which make a farthing.

defs: A small weight; one twentieth of a grain.
Anything very small; a minute object; a very little quantity or particle.
quotes: For in effect they be not worth a myte.
